title: ReproducibilityManifest
description: A small JSON file co-located with a paper's or replicator's code, describing the runtime environment and how to reproduce a claim. Linked from `replication` annotations via `reproducibility_manifest_uri` and from author-attached claim fields via `claim.reproducibility.manifest_uri`. See RRP-0019 for design rationale.

version.description: Manifest schema version. Pinned to 1.0 for v0.x.

title.description: Human-readable title for the manifest (e.g. 'Reproduction of Claim I.47').

claim_uri.description: Canonical URI of the claim this manifest reproduces. Typically https://rrxiv.com/claims/<claim_id>.

kind.description: Container or environment kind. v0.x recommends `docker`; the field is an open string so future manifests can add `conda`, `nix`, `modal`, etc.

image.description: Container image reference (e.g. `ghcr.io/example/repro:abc123`). Required when kind=docker.

digest.description: Content-addressable digest of the image. Strongly recommended.

data.description: Input data needed to run the reproduction. Optional — many reproductions have no external data.

ref.description: Git ref / HF revision / S3 version-ID when applicable.

entrypoint.description: Command to invoke inside the environment (e.g. `python run.py --seed 0`). Convention: deterministic; running it twice should produce identical `expected_outputs`.

path.description: Output path relative to the workspace inside the container.

hash.description: Expected SHA-256 of the file's bytes for byte-deterministic reproductions.

expected_outputs.description: Files (and optionally their hashes) the entrypoint is expected to produce. Empty for reproductions that only assert non-byte-level outcomes (e.g. plotted figures judged visually).

estimated_runtime_minutes.description: Rough runtime estimate so a reproducer can decide whether to run.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Rough cost estimate in USD (cloud-compute back-of-napkin).

notes.description: Free-form prose for nuances the structured fields can't capture.

examples.title: Reproduction of Proposition I.1 (equilateral triangle construction)
